basic_details.job_title: Indian Army SSC (Tech)-68 Men Recruitment 2026: Notification, Eligibility, Dates, Salary
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Application > Shortlisting > SSB > Medical > Merit List > Joining Letter
basic_details.short_information: Note: The notification states that the course details, tables, and highlights are for general awareness; the official notification published on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in is the only legally valid document for this course.

important_dates.interview_date: Five days between Oct 2026 and Dec 2026

application_fee.details: Not specified in the official notification. The notification does not mention any application fee or fee-payment process for the SSC (Tech)-68 Men course. Candidates should refer to the "Apply Online" section on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in at the time of application for the most current information.

qualification.details: Candidates who have passed the required engineering degree, or are in the final year of an engineering degree course in a notified stream, are eligible to apply. Final-year candidates must be able to submit proof of passing the engineering degree examination, along with marksheets of all semesters/years, by 01 April 2027, and produce the degree certificate within 12 weeks from the start of PCTA training. Candidates whose final year/final semester result is declared after 29 March 2027 are not eligible to apply for this course. Final-year candidates are provisionally allowed to appear for the SSB if their cumulative percentage of marks up to the 6th semester/3rd year (Engineering), 8th semester/4th year (B.Arch), or 2nd semester/1st year (M.Sc in a notified equivalent stream) is not below the approved cut-off percentage for their stream. After final results are declared, the cumulative percentage up to the final semester/year must also not fall below the approved cut-off, or the candidature will be cancelled. The degree must be from a university/institution recognized by an Act of Parliament/State Legislature or deemed university under UGC Act 1956, IIT Act 1961, AICTE Act 1987, NITSER Act 2007, or IIIT Act 2024. Degrees from foreign universities are not accepted.

salary_details.details: Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month (fixed) for Lieutenant to Brigadier Dearness Allowance: As applicable to civilian personnel, revised periodically Technical Allowance: ₹3,000/month (Tier-I) or ₹4,500/month (Tier-II) Dress Allowance: ₹20,000 per year Transport Allowance: ₹7,200/month + DA (higher TPTA cities) or ₹3,600/month + DA (other places), for Pay Level 10 and above Children Education Allowance: ₹2,250/month per child (for two eldest surviving children, Nursery to Class 12) Hostel Subsidy: As per the notification (for two eldest surviving children, Nursery to Class 12) Field Area / Hardship Allowances: Applicable depending on rank and posting location, as detailed in the notification Army Group Insurance (AGIF): Cadets in receipt of stipend are insured for ₹1.25 crore, with a monthly subscription of ₹12,500

selection_process: The Indian Army SSC (Tech)-68 Men selection follows this sequence:
Application > Shortlisting > SSB > Medical Examination > Merit List > Joining Letter
Applications are shortlisted stream-wise based on the vacancy-to-call-up ratio and the cumulative percentage of marks obtained up to the final semester/year
without the Army being required to state a reason. The shortlisting cut-off percentage is expected to be published in the first week of September 2026.
candidates log in to the website to select their SSB dates and centre on a first-come
the centre and date generally cannot be changed. This window is expected to open for two weeks in September 2026.
Shortlisted candidates are called to one of four Selection Centres: Prayagraj (UP)

and Jalandhar (Punjab). The SSB is a two-stage process. Candidates who clear Stage-1 proceed to Stage-2; those who do not clear Stage-1 are sent back the same day. The SSB interview lasts up to five days
likely held between October and December 2026. Candidates recommended after Stage-2 undergo a medical examination.

Medical standards and examination procedures are detailed on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. The SSB medical examination is valid for 270 days from the Special Medical Board (SMB) date to the PCTA joining date. Candidates already declared "Medically Fit" in a prior SSB medical (within the 270-day validity relative to 01 Apr 2027) need not undergo the medical again.
Qualifying the SSB interview alone does not confirm final selection. A stream-wise merit list is prepared based on SSB interview marks. In case of a tie in SSB marks
the older candidate is ranked higher; if age is also equal
the candidate with a higher percentage in the qualifying degree is ranked higher. Higher educational qualifications or past performance play no role in merit ranking.
how_to_apply: Click on "Officer Entry Application/Login," then click "Registration" (skip this step if already registered on the portal).
Fill the online registration form after carefully reading the instructions. Aadhaar number and matriculation certificate details are mandatory for registration.
After registering, click "Apply Online" under the Dashboard.
On the "Officers Selection – Eligibility" page, click "Apply" against the Short Service Commission Technical Course.
Fill in the Application Form across all sections: personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any). Click "Save & Continue" after each section.
Review all entries on the "Summary of your information" page.
Click "Submit" only after confirming all details are correct.
Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the final closure of the online application on the last day.
Important: No changes can be made to the submitted application after the closing date, and no representation on this will be entertained.
important_instructions: Any mismatch between the engineering stream on the degree certificate/marksheet and the stream selected in the online application will result in cancellation of candidature. Any variation in the candidate's name or parents' names between educational documents and the application requires an affidavit explaining the variation. CGPA/Grades must be converted to marks using the formula adopted by the candidate's own university before filling in "Marks obtained in Graduation." Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications result in cancellation. Candidates already serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing about applying, and their application must be countersigned by the Commanding Officer. Candidates who have ever been debarred by UPSC from any examination are not eligible. Candidates arrested, convicted by a criminal court, or involved in a criminal case are not eligible; discovery of such facts at any stage leads to cancellation/withdrawal. Candidates withdrawn from NDA, IMA, OTA, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, or any service training academy on disciplinary grounds are not eligible to apply. Requests for change of SSB interview date or centre will not be entertained or replied to. The notification is published in Hindi and English; in case of any discrepancy, the English version prevails.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army SSC Tech 68 Men: Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Queries related to allotment of centres, interview dates, merit list, or joining instructions are answered only through the "Feedback/Query" option on the official website; queries submitted less than 3 days before the application closing date are not entertained.

rows.event: Choice of SSB Dates & Centre Opens

rows.date: Five days between Oct 2026 and Dec 2026

content_sections.heading: Indian Army SSC Tech 68 Men Vacancy Details (Stream-Wise)
content_sections.text: A total of 350 vacancies (tentative, subject to change based on organizational requirements) have been notified across six core engineering streams: Each core stream includes several AICTE-approved equivalent stream names (for example, "Civil" includes Civil Engineering, Construction Engineering, Architecture Engineering, and related disciplines). Candidates must apply under the stream that exactly matches their degree certificate/marksheet nomenclature — any mismatch between the stream name on the degree and the stream selected in the online application results in cancellation of candidature. A candidate can apply for only one engineering stream/discipline; change of stream is not allowed at any stage of the selection process.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: A citizen of India, or A subject of Nepal, or A person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or East African countries (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia) and Vietnam, with the intention of permanently settling in India, provided a certificate of eligibility has been issued by the Government of India (not required for Gorkha subjects of Nepal). Open only to unmarried male candidates. Candidates must not marry until they complete the full training at the Officers Training Academy. Marrying during training or after applying (before joining) leads to cancellation/discharge. Candidates must be between 20 and 27 years as on 01 Apr 2027, i.e., born between 01 Apr 2000 and 31 Mar 2007 (both dates inclusive). Only the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) is accepted. No other document for age proof is accepted, and no request for change of recorded date of birth is considered later.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Candidates who have passed the required engineering degree , or are in the final year of an engineering degree course in a notified stream, are eligible to apply. Final-year candidates must be able to submit proof of passing the engineering degree examination, along with marksheets of all semesters/years, by 01 April 2027 , and produce the degree certificate within 12 weeks from the start of PCTA training. Candidates whose final year/final semester result is declared after 29 March 2027 are not eligible to apply for this course. Final-year candidates are provisionally allowed to appear for the SSB if their cumulative percentage of marks up to the 6th semester/3rd year (Engineering), 8th semester/4th year (B.Arch), or 2nd semester/1st year (M.Sc in a notified equivalent stream) is not below the approved cut-off percentage for their stream. After final results are declared, the cumulative percentage up to the final semester/year must also not fall below the approved cut-off, or the candidature will be cancelled. The degree must be from a university/institution recognized by an Act of Parliament/State Legislature or deemed university under UGC Act 1956, IIT Act 1961, AICTE Act 1987, NITSER Act 2007, or IIIT Act 2024. Degrees from foreign universities are not accepted .

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The Indian Army SSC (Tech)-68 Men selection follows this sequence: Application > Shortlisting > SSB > Medical Examination > Merit List > Joining Letter Applications are shortlisted stream-wise based on the vacancy-to-call-up ratio and the cumulative percentage of marks obtained up to the final semester/year, without the Army being required to state a reason. The shortlisting cut-off percentage is expected to be published in the first week of September 2026 . After shortlisting, candidates log in to the website to select their SSB dates and centre on a first-come, first-served basis. Once selected, the centre and date generally cannot be changed. This window is expected to open for two weeks in September 2026 . Shortlisted candidates are called to one of four Selection Centres: Prayagraj (UP), Bhopal (MP), Bengaluru (Karnataka), and Jalandhar (Punjab) . The SSB is a two-stage process. Candidates who clear Stage-1 proceed to Stage-2; those who do not clear Stage-1 are sent back the same day. The SSB interview lasts up to five days , likely held between October and December 2026 . Candidates recommended after Stage-2 undergo a medical examination. Medical standards and examination procedures are detailed on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. The SSB medical examination is valid for 270 days from the Special Medical Board (SMB) date to the PCTA joining date. Candidates already declared "Medically Fit" in a prior SSB medical (within the 270-day validity relative to 01 Apr 2027) need not undergo the medical again. Qualifying the SSB interview alone does not confirm final selection. A stream-wise merit list is prepared based on SSB interview marks. In case of a tie in SSB marks, the older candidate is ranked higher; if age is also equal, the candidate with a higher percentage in the qualifying degree is ranked higher. Higher educational qualifications or past performance play no role in merit ranking.

content_sections.heading: Training Details
content_sections.text: Training Academy: Officers Training Academy (OTA), Gaya, Bihar Duration: 49 weeks, from April 2027 to March 2028 Stipend during training: ₹56,100 per month (equal to the starting pay in Level-10 of the pay matrix) Rank on commissioning: Lieutenant, granted Short Service Commission on probation from the course start date or PCTA reporting date, whichever is later Ante-date seniority: One year ante-date seniority is granted from the date of commission Academic award: On successful completion of training, candidates are awarded a "Post Graduate Diploma in Military Studies & Defence Management" by Magadh University, Bodh Gaya Marriage restriction: Not permitted to marry during training; violation leads to discharge and recovery of expenses Cost of training recovery: If an Officer Cadet resigns/withdraws from training for reasons other than medical grounds, the cost of training (₹20,482.40 per week for the 01 Jul 2026–30 Jun 2027 period, as revised from time to time) must be refunded

content_sections.heading: Indian Army SSC Tech 68 Men Salary and Allowances
content_sections.text: Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month (fixed) for Lieutenant to Brigadier Dearness Allowance: As applicable to civilian personnel, revised periodically Technical Allowance: ₹3,000/month (Tier-I) or ₹4,500/month (Tier-II) Dress Allowance: ₹20,000 per year Transport Allowance: ₹7,200/month + DA (higher TPTA cities) or ₹3,600/month + DA (other places), for Pay Level 10 and above Children Education Allowance: ₹2,250/month per child (for two eldest surviving children, Nursery to Class 12) Hostel Subsidy: As per the notification (for two eldest surviving children, Nursery to Class 12) Field Area / Hardship Allowances: Applicable depending on rank and posting location, as detailed in the notification Army Group Insurance (AGIF): Cadets in receipt of stipend are insured for ₹1.25 crore, with a monthly subscription of ₹12,500

content_sections.heading: Type and Tenure of Commission
content_sections.text: Type of Commission: Short Service Commission Minimum engagement: 10 years Maximum engagement: 14 years (extendable by up to 4 years beyond 10, subject to eligibility) Release options: First — after 5 years; Second — after 10 years; Third — after 14 years Permanent Commission option: Can be considered in the 10th year of service, subject to eligibility and suitability as per prevailing terms

rows.promotion_criteria: On completion of 2 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 6 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 13 years reckonable commissioned service
On completion of 26 years reckonable commissioned service
On selection basis, subject to fulfilment of requisite service conditions

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Not specified in the official notification. The notification does not mention any application fee or fee-payment process for the SSC (Tech)-68 Men course. Candidates should refer to the "Apply Online" section on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in at the time of application for the most current information.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for SSB Interview
content_sections.text: Candidates must carry self-attested copies along with original documents to the Selection Centre. DigiLocker certificates are not accepted in place of originals unless accompanied by an authentication letter from the institution confirming only e-certificates were issued. One signed printout of the application form with a self-attested photograph affixed Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) and marksheet — the only accepted proof of date of birth and parents' names 12th class marksheet and certificate, or Diploma marksheet and certificate (for Diploma holders) Engineering Degree/Provisional Degree certificate issued by a recognized university (Provisional Degree must not be more than 6 months old at the time of joining the Academy) Marksheets of all semesters/years Certificate from the university's Controller of Examination/Registrar regarding the CGPA-to-percentage conversion formula Certificate confirming cumulative percentage up to the relevant semester/year (for final-year appearing candidates) Certificate from the Principal/Head of Institution confirming the candidate is in the final year and the result will be declared by 01 April 2027 Declaration by final-year candidates that they will submit proof of passing along with all marksheets by 01 April 2027 Documents related to civil/character verification An affidavit is required if there is any variation between the candidate's name/parents' names on educational documents and the online application

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Army SSC Tech 68 Men Online
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: www.joinindianarmy.nic.in Click on "Officer Entry Application/Login," then click "Registration" (skip this step if already registered on the portal). Fill the online registration form after carefully reading the instructions. Aadhaar number and matriculation certificate details are mandatory for registration. After registering, click "Apply Online" under the Dashboard. On the "Officers Selection – Eligibility" page, click "Apply" against the Short Service Commission Technical Course. Fill in the Application Form across all sections: personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any). Click "Save & Continue" after each section. Review all entries on the "Summary of your information" page. Click "Submit" only after confirming all details are correct. Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the final closure of the online application on the last day. Important: No changes can be made to the submitted application after the closing date, and no representation on this will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions to Note
content_sections.text: Any mismatch between the engineering stream on the degree certificate/marksheet and the stream selected in the online application will result in cancellation of candidature. Any variation in the candidate's name or parents' names between educational documents and the application requires an affidavit explaining the variation. CGPA/Grades must be converted to marks using the formula adopted by the candidate's own university before filling in "Marks obtained in Graduation." Only one online application is allowed per candidate; multiple applications result in cancellation. Candidates already serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing about applying, and their application must be countersigned by the Commanding Officer. Candidates who have ever been debarred by UPSC from any examination are not eligible. Candidates arrested, convicted by a criminal court, or involved in a criminal case are not eligible; discovery of such facts at any stage leads to cancellation/withdrawal. Candidates withdrawn from NDA, IMA, OTA, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, or any service training academy on disciplinary grounds are not eligible to apply. Requests for change of SSB interview date or centre will not be entertained or replied to. The notification is published in Hindi and English; in case of any discrepancy, the English version prevails .

content_sections.heading: Travelling Allowance for SSB Interview
content_sections.text: Candidates appearing for the SSB interview for the first time for this type of commission are entitled to AC-3 Tier railway fare (or equivalent bus fare, including reservation/sleeper charges) within Indian limits, for to-and-fro travel. Candidates reappearing for the same type of commission are not entitled to travelling allowance on subsequent occasions.

faq.question: Q1. What is the Indian Army SSC (Tech)-68 Men course?
faq.answer: It is the 68th Short Service Commission (Technical) course for unmarried male engineering graduates, commencing in April 2027 at the Officers Training Academy, Gaya, Bihar.

faq.question: Q2. What is the age limit for SSC Tech 68 Men?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 20 and 27 years old as on 01 April 2027, i.e., born between 01 April 2000 and 31 March 2007 (both dates inclusive).

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for Indian Army SSC Tech 68 Men 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 07 August 2026 at 1500 hrs. Applications opened on 09 July 2026 at 1500 hrs.

faq.question: Q4. How many vacancies are there in SSC Tech 68 Men?
faq.answer: A total of 350 tentative vacancies are notified across Civil (75), Computer Science (60), Electrical (33), Electronics (64), Mechanical (101), and Miscellaneous Engineering streams (17).

faq.question: Q5. Can final-year engineering students apply?
faq.answer: Yes, final-year students can apply, provided their final year/final semester result is not declared after 29 March 2027, and they meet the cumulative cut-off percentage conditions described in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary during training and after commissioning?
faq.answer: Cadets receive a stipend of ₹56,100 per month during the 49-week training. After commissioning as Lieutenant, the approximate CTC is ₹17–18 lakh per annum (excluding free medical cover and one home-town travel per year).

faq.question: Q7. Where are the SSB interview centres for SSC Tech 68 Men?
faq.answer: Prayagraj (Uttar Pradesh), Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh), Bengaluru (Karnataka), and Jalandhar (Punjab).

faq.question: Q8. Is there an application fee for SSC Tech 68 Men?
faq.answer: The official notification does not specify any application fee for this course.

faq.question: Q9. What is the training duration and location?
faq.answer: Training lasts 49 weeks, from April 2027 to March 2028, at the Officers Training Academy, Gaya, Bihar.

faq.question: Q10. Can SSC Tech officers get a Permanent Commission?
faq.answer: Yes, officers may be considered for Permanent Commission in the 10th year of service, subject to eligibility and suitability as per terms issued from time to time.
